title: Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese

seo.title: Palak Paneer Recipe: Easy Indian Spinach Cottage Cheese Curry
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Palak Paneer at home with our easy, authentic Indian recipe. This creamy spinach and cottage cheese curry is perfect for vegetarians!
longTailKeywords: how to make Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese
easy Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese recipe
authentic Indian Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese
Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese step by step
best Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese at home
seo.h1: Authentic Palak Paneer Recipe: Indian Spinach Cottage Cheese Curry
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Palak Paneer
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Palak Paneer
Pro Tips for the Best Palak Paneer

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, green Palak Paneer curry with cubes of paneer, garnished with cream and cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this delicious homemade Palak Paneer, a classic Indian spinach and cottage cheese curry.

description: Palak Paneer is a beloved Indian dish featuring tender cubes of paneer Indian cottage cheese simmered in a vibrant, creamy spinach gravy. This wholesome and flavorful curry is a staple in vegetarian Indian cuisine, offering a rich blend of spices and a comforting texture. It pairs wonderfully with naan, roti, or rice for a satisfying meal.

ingredients.notes: slit or finely chopped, adjust to taste

instructions.title: Blanch the Spinach
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add the washed spinach leaves and blanch for 1-2 minutes until they wilt. Immediately transfer the blanched spinach to a bowl of ice-cold water to stop the cooking process and retain its vibrant green color. Drain well.

instructions.title: Prepare the Spinach Puree
instructions.text: Once the blanched spinach is cool, squeeze out any excess water. Transfer it to a blender along with the green chilies and blend into a smooth puree. Add a little water if needed to achieve a fine consistency. Set aside.

instructions.title: Saute Paneer Cubes (Optional)
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil or ghee in a pan over medium heat. Lightly pan-fry the paneer cubes until they are golden brown on all sides. This step is optional but adds a nice texture. Remove and set aside. You can also directly add raw paneer.

instructions.title: Prepare the Aromatic Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining oil or ghee. Once hot, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add the finely chopped onions and saute until they turn golden brown. Then, add the ginger garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Cook the Spices and Tomatoes
instructions.text: Add the pureed tomatoes to the pan and cook until the oil starts to separate from the mixture, stirring occasionally. Now, add turmeric powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes to allow the spices to meld.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Pour the spinach puree into the pan with the cooked masala. Mix everything thoroughly. Add about 1/2 cup of water to adjust the consistency. Bring it to a gentle simmer, then add the fried paneer cubes (or raw paneer), garam masala, and crushed Kasuri Methi. Stir gently.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Cover the pan and let the Palak Paneer simmer for 5-7 minutes on low heat, allowing the flavors to infuse. If using, stir in the cream or cashew paste for extra richness and a smoother texture. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander leaves before serving hot.

tips: To ensure a vibrant green color, always blanch spinach quickly and immediately plunge it into ice-cold water before pureeing.
For a richer and creamier Palak Paneer, you can add a tablespoon of cashew paste or fresh cream at the end of cooking.
If you dont have fresh paneer, you can use store-bought paneer. For best results, soak it in warm water for 15-20 minutes before adding to the curry to make it soft.
article: Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese is a beloved Indian Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
